summary: Matched nearly all of src/melee/it/items/itegg.c, adding a 312-line decompilation for Egg item behavior and reaching 99.89% file score. The PR also tightened symbol visibility for many itegg-local functions and one local sdata2 float, refined EggVars from an opaque filler/heal field into named fields, renamed the Item union member from Egg to egg, and updated heal-value access in it_8026B47C. The author explicitly called out uncertainty around type punning in it_802891E0 and whether EggVars needed adjustment; follow-up comments indicate agreement that the reused storage was the same struct and note the file was very close to linking.
what_changed: Implemented src/melee/it/items/itegg.c from an effectively empty file to a 312-line decompilation covering Egg item state table setup, spawn helper it_80288C88, state callbacks, breaking/landing behavior, heal/effect behavior, and forwarding wrappers such as it_802893D4 through it_802895A8.
Added static prototypes for many local itegg callbacks and defined ItemStateTable it_803F5988 with seven state entries mapping animation/state IDs to callback triples.
Added a public declaration for it_80288C88 in itegg.h and included dolphin mtx forward declarations for Vec3.
Changed many itegg functions in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from global to local scope, matching the new static C declarations.
Changed sdata2 symbol it_804DC9B8 to local label @180, preserving address and float size but no longer exporting it as a global named object.
Refined EggVars in itCommonItems.h from an opaque u8 filler[0x60] plus xE34_heal into named fields: bool x0, int rand_max, padding from 0x8 to 0x60, and int heal_amount.
Renamed the Item union member from xDD4_itemVar.Egg to xDD4_itemVar.egg in src/melee/it/types.h and updated callers that use EggVars.
Updated it_8026B47C healing-item lookup so Pokemon_Lucky_Egg returns temp_item->xDD4_itemVar.egg.heal_amount instead of xE34_heal.
Temporarily commented out several ittomato.c accesses that previously reached into Egg.filler[4], likely because the EggVars layout/member rename made those raw filler accesses invalid or suspect.
smart_moves: Used static functions and local symbol scope together: callbacks that are only used inside itegg.c were made static in C and marked scope:local in symbols.txt, reducing accidental external API surface.
Converted the Egg item variable struct from opaque filler-based access to named semantic fields where evidence existed, improving future readability and reducing reliance on magic offsets.
Preserved exact offsets in EggVars with explicit padding after the named fields, allowing decomp code to name known members without disturbing the underlying item variable layout.

Reused the same EggVars storage for multiple Egg-like contexts after discussion; the author identified suspicious type punning in it_802891E0 before changing the struct, and the follow-up comment says, 'Ah okay, so they are the same struct.'
Kept unresolved or questionable tomato-side bitfield/filler accesses commented rather than inventing a possibly wrong replacement member name.
Used PAD_STACK in functions such as it_80288E6C and the wrapper callbacks where the diff shows stack-shape padding was needed for matching.
decomp_lessons: When an item variable union member is reused by several related item behaviors, first determine whether the observed type punning is actually shared struct storage before splitting or renaming fields.
Opaque filler arrays should be gradually replaced with named fields only when offsets and semantics are supported by usage; preserve unknown regions with explicit padding.
If a field rename breaks unrelated m2c output that was poking into filler bytes, it is safer to leave those accesses commented or unresolved than to assign unsupported semantics.
For item state callbacks, matching often benefits from making internal callbacks static and aligning symbols.txt scope to local.
Small item files can be brought close to fully linked status by combining state table reconstruction, callback matching, symbol-scope cleanup, and struct cleanup in the same PR.
Review comments may contain important type-layout guidance even when formal review comments are absent; here the useful feedback was in issue comments rather than code review threads.
naming_conventions: EggVars fields were renamed to lower_snake_case semantic names such as rand_max and heal_amount instead of address-derived names like xE34_heal.
The Item union member for EggVars was changed from capitalized Egg to lowercase egg, matching the style used by direct variable-style union members in the touched code.
Unknown padding inside EggVars was named _8 with an offset comment, preserving layout documentation without inventing semantics.
Struct field comments use offset notation such as /* +0 */, /* +4 */, /* +8 */, and /* +60 */.
The file retained address-style function names such as it_80288C88 and it_802891E0 because no stronger semantic names were established in the PR evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Marked many callbacks static and changed corresponding symbols.txt entries from scope:global to scope:local.
Introduced PAD_STACK(8) in it_80288E6C and PAD_STACK(4) in several forwarding wrapper functions to satisfy stack/layout matching.
Used explicit zeroing patterns for Vec3 and velocity fields, including chained assignments to x/y/z, consistent with matching-oriented C.
Used ternary form in it_802891E0 after pre-decrementing egg.rand_max: return ip->xDD4_itemVar.egg.rand_max > 0 ? false : true.
Maintained address-named local label @180 for a local sdata2 float instead of keeping the previous global symbol name it_804DC9B8.
Used casts such as Item_80268E5C((Item_GObj*) gobj, 2, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE) where needed by the matched code shape.

review_feedback.author: wyatt-avilla
review_feedback.text: The PR body says it was marked draft because type punning in it_802891E0 made the author think EggVars might need changing and they wanted a second opinion.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Commented: 'Ah okay, so they are the same struct.' This supports the EggVars/shared-storage interpretation, but the context excerpt does not include the full discussion.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Commented: 'Hm, very close to linking the file.' This reflects that the file was nearly complete but still not fully linked/matched at merge, consistent with the 99.89% score.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching EggVars or xDD4_itemVar.egg to see whether rand_max, x0, and heal_amount were renamed or further validated.
Find later PRs touching src/melee/it/items/ittomato.c to see how the commented Egg.filler[4] accesses were resolved.
Search for PRs that fully link src/melee/it/items/itegg.c after this 99.89% match.
Search for additional comments around it_802891E0 type punning to recover the missing reasoning behind the shared EggVars conclusion.
Look for other item files that changed symbol scopes from global to local when static callbacks were introduced.
